summary: tempo-layout exfiltrates host, network, and environment data to interactsh OOB server on install
details: The package's preinstall lifecycle hook runs `node poc.js`, which executes `ipconfig /all` on Windows or `ip a && cat /etc/resolv.conf` on Linux to capture the installer's network configuration, then collects hostname, username, install directory, network interfaces, the full list of environment variable names, and all `npm_*` environment values. This data is JSON-serialized and POSTed over HTTPS to a hardcoded interactsh subdomain (`d8a5d9pon5bugoc35cngp9hcregcqyezu.oast.me`), with an additional DNS callback encoding hostname and username. The package additionally self-describes as a dependency-confusion proof-of-concept targeting a private `@livingdesign/react` namespace, with `index.js` printing a warning that the package was installed from public npm instead of a private registry. Regardless of any stated bug-bounty framing, any installer outside the researcher's specific target experiences unauthorized reconnaissance and exfiltration of host and npm-config data on `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
